metadata_field_used: law (full name) + zakon (short code) — both present on every vector

missing_from_corpus.law: zakon o bezbednosti saobracaja na putevima
missing_from_corpus.impact: Queries about traffic violations, DUI, speed limits, license revocation return empty Pinecone results — AI answers from training data only, no grounding
Queries about foreign exchange, money transfers abroad, currency conversion return empty Pinecone results
Work permit queries return empty Pinecone results
Visa, residence permit, and foreigner status queries return empty Pinecone results
Asylum queries return empty Pinecone results

missing_from_corpus.law: zakon o pristupu informacijama od javnog znacaja
missing_from_corpus.impact: FOIA-equivalent queries (public information access) return empty Pinecone results

orphaned_vectors.name: zakon o zastiti podataka o licnosti
orphaned_vectors.issue: Indexed in Pinecone but ZERO entries in LAW_HINTS route to it. No keyword triggers (GDPR, licni podaci, obrada podataka, etc.) are defined. Content reachable only via non-filtered semantic fallback — law-specific accuracy severely degraded.

orphaned_vectors.name: web3_zdi_mca namespace
orphaned_vectors.issue: Entire namespace exists in Pinecone but retrieve.py never queries it. Metadata schema differs (fields: izvor, naslov, propis, tekst, tip — not the standard law/zakon/article schema). These 479 vectors are completely unreachable by any user query.
orphaned_vectors.priority: MEDIUM — investigate whether to integrate or delete

bugs_found.description: LAW_HINTS maps ZPDG triggers to the string 'ZPDG'. retrieve.py passes this as filter={"law":{"$eq":"ZPDG"}}. But vectors in Pinecone have law='zakon o porezu na dohodak gradjana', NOT 'ZPDG'. The filter silently returns 0 results. Expansion queries (_ZPDG_TERMINI via _semanticka_pretraga(term, 3, 'ZPDG')) also fail for the same reason. ZPDG content is found only via the non-filtered semantic fallback — unguaranteed and diluted by other law results.
bugs_found.fix: Change LAW_HINTS value from 'ZPDG' to 'zakon o porezu na dohodak gradjana'. Also fix _ZPDG_TERMINI expansion call to use the full name.

P0: zakon o bezbednosti saobracaja na putevima (ZBSN) — traffic law, very common queries
zakon o deviznom poslovanju (ZDF) — foreign exchange law
zakon o zaposljavanju stranaca (ZRDS) — work permits for foreigners
zakon o pristupu informacijama od javnog znacaja (ZPPI) — public information access
P1: zakon o upravnim sporovima (ZUS): only 161 chunks — may be incomplete; full text ~200 clanova expected ~400+ chunks
zakon o nasledjivanju (ZN): 341 chunks — verify completeness against official PDF
P2: zakon o hipoteci — mortgage law, common in real estate disputes
zakon o planiranju i izgradnji — construction/zoning, high query volume expected
zakon o autorskom i srodnim pravima — copyright law (IP practices)
zakon o prekrsajima — misdemeanor procedure (precedes ZKP for minor offenses)
zakon o javnim nabavkama — public procurement (relevant for lawyers advising companies)
zakon o elektronskim komunikacijama — telecom/data law (overlaps with ZDI scope)

notes: Both metadata fields exist on every vector: 'law' (full Serbian name) and 'zakon' (uppercase short code, e.g. 'ZOO'). retrieve.py uses 'law' for semantic filter and 'zakon' for article-level fetch.
The 17 laws sum to exactly 17,710 — full default namespace accounted for. No unknown/stray vectors.
KZ and ZKP are separate laws with separate chunks (KZ=criminal code, ZKP=criminal procedure code) — correct architecture.
ZPD (Zakon o privrednim drustvima) has the highest chunk count at 2249, suggesting a large and complex law or very fine-grained chunking.
web3_zdi_mca namespace (479 vectors) has a different metadata schema (izvor/naslov/propis/tekst/tip) — possibly ingested by a different pipeline. Not integrated into main retrieval.
pred_* namespaces (5 vectors each, 6 total = 30 vectors) are per-case uploaded document namespaces for document-level RAG — these are correct and expected.

Audit method: targeted filter queries per known law value. Zero-vector dummy queries with law filter to count matches. All 17710 default-namespace vectors accounted for. NOT a full sequential fetch — counts are exact due to Pinecone top_k=10000 filter behavior.
